evidence_excerpt: At the age of 27 she died of acute bronchopneumonia after a 6-year history of progressive neurodegeneration.

quality_of_life.evidence_excerpt: Persistent visual and auditory hallucinations and paranoia with facial, cervical and limb dystonia, spasticity, ataxic gait; symptoms progressed.

timeline_events.evidence_excerpt: Drug therapy with neuroleptics led to a deterioration of the paranoid symptoms and mobility and was therefore discontinued.
In the following year the symptoms progressed and she became bedridden.
Developed acute bronchopneumonia (reported as cause of death).
At the age of 27 she died of acute bronchopneumonia.

notes_on_uncertainty: Exact diagnosis date is not explicitly documented; index time approximated to when MRI showed decreased T2 signal in globus pallidus/substantia nigra supporting NBIA-1 (around age 25). Event timings are categorized approximately relative to this reference.
